verdict.formatted_prompt:
You are an expert evaluator. You will be given two outputs (A and B) that
solve the same task. Compare them and state your preference for each quality.

DOMAIN: user_onboarding
TASK: Redesign enterprise user onboarding to reduce time-to-value from 2 weeks to 3 days. The onboarding recipe demands adaptive, role-based tutorials. The content publishing recipe demands accessibility and SEO. The testing strategy recipe demands comprehensive coverage. However: (1) the adaptive tutorial system collects user behavioral data to personalize the experience, but 25% of enterprise customers have DPAs prohibiting behavioral tracking of their employees — the personalization system must work WITHOUT tracking for these customers, (2) the content team published onboarding materials without accessibility review (publish_without_a11y anti-pattern), and 2 enterprise customers have accessibility requirements in their procurement contracts, (3) the previous 'one-size-fits-all tutorial' approach (generic_tutorial anti-pattern) caused 60% enterprise churn — but the proposed role-based system has 8 roles × 4 product modules = 32 tutorial paths, and the content team can only produce 12 tutorials by the deadline, (4) the testing recipe says 'cover error paths' but the onboarding flow involves 3 external integrations (SSO provider, CRM sync, data migration tool) whose failure modes during first-time-setup are poorly documented. Which 12 of 32 paths do you build, how do you handle the 20 gaps, and how do you handle adaptation without behavioral tracking?

═══════════════════ OUTPUT A ═══════════════════
{"response": "1️⃣ **Adaptive tutorial without tracking** – Build a rule‑based engine that relies only on explicit user input (role, module choice, short pre‑setup questionnaire) and static company attributes. Add a "No‑Tracking Mode" flag to disable analytics cookies and server logs for DPAs that forbid behavioral data.

2️⃣ **Accessibility & SEO compliance** – Insert an automated WCAG 2.1 AA linting step (axe-core, Lighthouse) into the content pipeline. For the two customers with accessibility clauses, run a rapid manual audit on their published pages and fix critical issues before launch. Ensure all tutorials use semantic HTML, alt text, proper heading hierarchy, ARIA labels where needed, and include SEO meta tags and structured data.

3️⃣ **Selecting 12 of 32 tutorial paths** – Prioritize the paths that address the highest churn drivers: Admins & Managers in Core Modules (Analytics, Billing, User Management). Pick 8 paths covering these roles across modules. Add 4 additional paths focused on high‑volume integrations (SSO, CRM sync, Data Migration) to satisfy testing requirements. The remaining 20 gaps are handled by:
   • Modular micro‑tutorials that can be composed on demand.
   • A fallback "Quick Start" path with optional role anchors.
   • In‑app prompts that guide users to the relevant module after initial setup.

4️⃣ **Testing external integrations** – Map failure modes for SSO, CRM sync, and Data Migration (authentication errors, timeouts, data mismatches). Create contract‑based test suites (Pact) that simulate provider responses. Include negative tests in the 12 selected paths to trigger these failures, ensuring comprehensive error‑path coverage.

5️⃣ **Timeline** – Week 1: finalize rule engine and accessibility/SEO checklist; Week 2: produce 12 tutorials with adaptive logic and run automated tests; Week 3: manual QA, accessibility review, stakeholder sign‑off. This plan reduces time‑to‑value to ~3 days while meeting all constraints.", "confidence": "0.95", "internal_reasoning": "I mapped each constraint to a concrete action, prioritized high‑impact tutorial paths, and outlined testing and compliance steps based on industry best practices.", "_metrics": {"tokens": 1615, "prompt_tokens": 1111, "completion_tokens": 504, "latency_ms": 5490.74, "cost_usd": 0.000339, "model": "openai/gpt-oss-20b", "provider": "lm_studio"}, "formatted_prompt": "Produce the FINAL structured output for this complex task.

DOMAIN: general
TASK: {'domain': 'user_onboarding', 'task': "Redesign enterprise user onboarding to reduce time-to-value from 2 weeks to 3 days. The onboarding recipe demands adaptive, role-based tutorials. The content publishing recipe demands accessibility and SEO. The testing strategy recipe demands comprehensive coverage. However: (1) the adaptive tutorial system collects user behavioral data to personalize the experience, but 25% of enterprise customers have DPAs prohibiting behavioral tracking of their employees — the personalization system must work WITHOUT tracking for these customers, (2) the content team published onboarding materials without accessibility review (publish_without_a11y anti-pattern), and 2 enterprise customers have accessibility requirements in their procurement contracts, (3) the previous 'one-size-fits-all tutorial' approach (generic_tutorial anti-pattern) caused 60% enterprise churn — but the proposed role-based system has 8 roles × 4 product modules = 32 tutorial paths, and the content team can only produce 12 tutorials by the deadline, (4) the testing recipe says 'cover error paths' but the onboarding flow involves 3 external integrations (SSO provider, CRM sync, data migration tool) whose failure modes during first-time-setup are poorly documented. Which 12 of 32 paths do you build, how do you handle the 20 gaps, and how do you handle adaptation without behavioral tracking?"}

SOLUTION:
1. **Adaptive tutorial without tracking** – Build a rule‑based engine that uses only explicit user input (role selection, module choice, short pre‑setup questionnaire) and static company attributes to decide which micro‑tutorials to show. Provide a "No‑Tracking Mode" flag so analytics cookies and server logs are disabled for DPAs that forbid behavioral data.

2. **Accessibility & SEO compliance** – Integrate an automated WCAG 2.1 AA linting step into the content pipeline (axe‑core, Lighthouse). For the two customers with accessibility clauses, run a rapid manual audit on their published pages and fix critical issues before launch. Ensure all tutorials use semantic HTML, alt text, proper heading hierarchy, ARIA labels where needed, and include SEO meta tags and structured data.

3. **Selecting 12 of 32 tutorial paths** – Prioritize the paths that address the highest churn drivers: Admins & Managers in Core Modules (Analytics, Billing, User Management). Pick 8 paths covering these roles across modules. Add 4 additional paths focused on high‑volume integrations (SSO, CRM sync, Data Migration) to satisfy testing requirements. The remaining 20 gaps are handled by:
   * Modular micro‑tutorials that can be composed on demand.
   * A fallback “Quick Start” path with optional role anchors.
   * In‑app prompts that guide users to the relevant module after initial setup.

4. **Testing external integrations** – Map failure modes for SSO, CRM sync, and Data Migration (authentication errors, timeouts, data mismatches). Create contract‑based test suites (Pact) that simulate provider responses. Include negative tests in the 12 selected paths to trigger these failures, ensuring comprehensive error‑path coverage.

5. **Timeline** – Week 1: finalize rule engine and accessibility/SEO checklist; Week 2: produce 12 tutorials with adaptive logic and run automated tests; Week 3: manual QA, accessibility review, stakeholder sign‑off.

DOMAIN: general
TASK: {'domain': 'user_onboarding', 'task': "Redesign enterprise user onboarding to reduce time-to-value from 2 weeks to 3 days. The onboarding recipe demands adaptive, role-based tutorials. The content publishing recipe demands accessibility and SEO. The testing strategy recipe demands comprehensive coverage. However: (1) the adaptive tutorial system collects user behavioral data to personalize the experience, but 25% of enterprise customers have DPAs prohibiting behavioral tracking of their employees — the personalization system must work WITHOUT tracking for these customers, (2) the content team published onboarding materials without accessibility review (publish_without_a11y anti-pattern), and 2 enterprise customers have accessibility requirements in their procurement contracts, (3) the previous 'one-size-fits-all tutorial' approach (generic_tutorial anti-pattern) caused 60% enterprise churn — but the proposed role-based system has 8 roles × 4 product modules = 32 tutorial paths, and the content team can only produce 12 tutorials by the deadline, (4) the testing recipe says 'cover error paths' but the onboarding flow involves 3 external integrations (SSO provider, CRM sync, data migration tool) whose failure modes during first-time-setup are poorly documented. Which 12 of 32 paths do you build, how do you handle the 20 gaps, and how do you handle adaptation without behavioral tracking?"}

COMPOSED SOLUTION:
1️⃣ **Select 12 core paths** – Pick the four highest‑impact roles (Admin, Manager, Analyst, Support) and pair each with the two most critical modules (User Management & Data Integration). That gives 8 paths. Add Developer & Auditor for User Management and Trainer & Ops for Data Integration to reach 12.

2️⃣ **Close the remaining 20 gaps** – Build a *modular tutorial library* of reusable cards, videos, and docs. A lightweight rule engine stitches these modules at runtime based on role, integration status, and company size, creating any of the 32 paths without writing each one from scratch. For unbuilt paths provide a concise “quick‑start” guide linking to relevant modules.

3️⃣ **Adaptation without behavioral tracking** – Use deterministic inputs: selected role, chosen integrations, org size, and local session state (browser storage). If DPAs forbid tracking, default to the role‑based path; otherwise enrich with micro‑learning suggestions derived from observed progress within the same session. No cross‑session or external analytics are used.

4️⃣ **Accessibility & SEO** – Run an a11y audit on the 12 core tutorials (axe, Lighthouse). Ensure WCAG 2.1 AA compliance: semantic HTML, ARIA labels, captions, keyboard navigation, and alt text. For SEO, embed keyword‑rich meta tags, structured data (JSON‑LD), and descriptive URLs.

5️⃣ **Testing strategy** – Create a risk‑based matrix for the three external integrations. Define four failure modes per integration (auth error, timeout, data mismatch, permission denied) → 12 error paths. Automate UI tests with Playwright/Cypress to simulate each failure and verify graceful fallbacks. Add unit tests for the rule engine and dynamic assembly logic.

6️⃣ **Timeline** – Week 1: finalize role‑module mapping, build modular library, audit a11y/SEO. Week 2: develop rule engine & wizard, produce 12 core tutorials. Week 3: implement automated tests, run integration failure simulations, iterate. Deliverable ready for deployment within 3 days of activation.

This plan reduces time‑to‑value to 3 days, respects DPAs and accessibility contracts, mitigates churn from generic tutorials, and ensures comprehensive error coverage.

COMPOSITION STRATEGY:
N/A

BRAIN METRICS:
Skills composed: []
Anti-patterns avoided: []
